summary:
Elevated \(\alpha\)fetoprotein levels indicate neural tube defects, making it the most reliable marker compared to others mentioned.
The optimal test for diagnosing neural tube defects involves monitoring increased \(\alpha\)fetoprotein concentrations during prenatal screening. Summary: Increased \(\alpha\)-fetoprotein indicates neural tube defects; thus, it serves optimally for diagnosis when comparing tested markers. Key point highlighted: reliability of \(\alpha\)-fetoprotein elevation in detection.
